items.title: US annual PCE inflation holds steady at 2.8% in February as expected
items.summary: Inflation in the United States, as measured by the change in the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Price Index, held steady at 2.8% in February, the US Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) reported on Thursday. This print came in line with the market expectation.

items.title: Iran's President Pezeshkian: Attacks on Lebanon deem ceasefire negotiations meaningless
items.summary: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian said on Thursday that Israeli strikes on Lebanon would deem negotiations meaningless because they violate the ceasefire agreement, per Reuters.

items.title: NZD: RBNZ warns on upside inflation risks – BNY
items.summary: BNY’s Head of Markets Macro Strategy Bob Savage reports that Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) Governor Anna Breman signaled readiness to hike rates if core inflation accelerates, citing upside risks from higher fuel prices tied to Middle East tensions.

items.title: UK: February rebound but 2026 outlook soft – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank’s Sanjay Raja expects UK GDP to rebound by 0.2% m-o-m in February after a flat January, with balanced risks around the nowcast. The bank’s models also point to a possible upward revision to January.

items.title: Oil: Ceasefire fragility tempers price reaction – Rabobank
items.summary: Rabobank strategist Molly Schwartz notes that crude Oil’s initial sharp drop on the Iran ceasefire headlines has been only partially reversed, despite renewed tensions and the Strait of Hormuz re-closing.

items.title: US Dollar Index (DXY) picks up to 99.00 with Iran’s ceasefire into question
items.summary: The US Dollar (USD) has trimmed some losses to consolidate right above the 99.00 level on Thursday, after bouncing from lows at 98.50 on Wednesday. The safe-haven US Dollar has picked up as investors come to terms with the fragility of the ceasefire in Iran.

items.title: SEK: Riksbank seen steady despite soft CPI – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank analyst Antje Praefcke says weaker-than-expected Swedish inflation does not give the central bank of Sweden, Riksbank a reason to change its stance.

items.title: Dow Jones futures slip due to fading US-Iran ceasefire optimism
items.summary: Dow Jones futures fall 0.36% to near 47,950 during European hours on Thursday, ahead of the regular opening in the United States (US). Meanwhile, S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures also lose 0.38% and 0.37% to near 6,800 and 25,000, respectively, at the time of writing.

items.title: Australian Dollar faces pressure as Iran truce uncertainty revives risk-off impulse slightly
items.summary: The Australian Dollar (AUD) trades lower against its major currency peers, is down 0.2% to near 0.7030 vs. the US Dollar (USD) during the European trading session on Thursday.

items.title: BoJ’s Ueda says Japan's financial conditions remain accommodative
items.summary: Bank of Japan (BoJ) Governor Kazuo Ueda said that real interest rates are clearly negative and keeping the country's financial conditions accommodative, Reuters reported on Thursday.

items.title: US President Donald Trump warns of stronger military response if Iran deal fails
items.summary: US President Donald Trump said on Thursday that US forces will remain deployed around Iran until a final agreement is fully implemented. He emphasized that the Strait of Hormuz must remain open and safe.

items.title: US Dollar Index moves little due to renewed safe-haven demand
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the US Dollar (USD) against six major currencies, is remaining flat after three days of losses and hovering around 99.00 during the Asian hours on Wednesday.
